name: traceweaver-core
description: Systems-engineering authority control and advisory automation for agentic software work.

interface.shortDescription: Authority, requirements, and traceability controls for agentic engineering.
interface.longDescription: TraceWeaver Core repackages the selected Compound Engineering method with systems-engineering authority. The alpha runtime focuses on requirement quality review, no-orphan traceability checks, root requirements and matrix bootstrap, advisory controlled automation, and dark-behavior routing before planning, implementation, review, and release claims.
defaultPrompt: Use the tw-requirements-review skill for requirements or planning authority review.
Use the tw-strategy skill for STRATEGY.md grounding as source evidence only; strategy does not override requirements or the Intent Contract.
Use the tw-ideate skill for generated/ranked ideas as source evidence only before grilling, brainstorming, or requirements review.
Use the tw-grill skill after ideation and before brainstorming when one selected idea needs deeper interview; its output is source evidence, not authority.
Use the tw-authority-gate skill before implementation starts on meaningful behavior.
Use the tw-traceability-check skill during plan, code, document, or release-evidence review.
Use the tw-auto skill for advisory controlled autonomy; it preserves the CE-style loop while adding authority, traceability, verification, validation, and dark-behavior controls.
The packaged lfg name delegates to tw-auto and must stop before commit, push, or PR creation in this alpha.
TraceWeaver Codex packaging defaults to gpt-5.5 with medium reasoning; Claude packaging defaults to Sonnet where the platform surface supports it. Runtime enforcement claims remain held until proven.
